message:
chore(beads): close bd-18qq + bd-1j4 (all children done); normalize bd-1y7 status

Two EPICs had every child and dependency closed but the parent was
never flipped from open:

- bd-18qq (Testing + Fault Injection Campaign): all 7 dotted children
  (.1 adversarial pointers, .2 allocator fragmentation storms,
  .3 thread preemption storms, .4 UAF detection E2E, .5 double-free
  under concurrent stress E2E, .6 ABI-safe strict mode under stress,
  .7 POSIX conformance for 250 symbols) closed by their respective
  owners. Epic had no direct `dependencies` blockers.
- bd-1j4 (Hard Parts Roadmap: rtld/CRT + NSS/locale/resolver): all 5
  dotted children (.1 CRT phase-1, .2 RTLD phase-1, .3 NSS/resolver,
  .4 locale/iconv, .5 integrated battery) plus the 14 dep-graph
  blockers closed. One of those blockers, bd-1y7 (Resolver phase-1
  UDP DNS + resolv.conf parser + timeout/retry), had been left at
  `status: "done"` by opus-tsm with a filled close_reason pointing at
  bd-zh1y.7.2 as evidence — that's a br enum-drift bug. Normalized it
  to `status: "closed"` in the same push so bd-1j4's blocker graph
  correctly evaluates as clear.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fixture churn without control creates non-deterministic regressions and review ambiguity.

Scope:
- Establish deterministic golden capture/update protocol with checksums and approval metadata.
- Build coverage regression gate that fails when conformance coverage or expected behavior drifts unexpectedly.

Deliverables:
1) Golden update protocol and checksum policy.
2) Coverage drift detector integrated into CI.
3) Triage report format for behavior deltas.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Golden updates are deterministic and reviewable.
- Coverage regressions fail CI with actionable diagnostics.

Rationale:
- Prevents silent erosion of conformance quality.

- Unit tests for checksum and drift detection logic.
- E2E tests for expected pass/fail drift scenarios.

 {"id":"bd-15q","title":"Kernel: Localization fixed-point chooser (design)","description":"Design Atiyah-Bott localization style policy selection.

Idea:
- Precompute a small set of fixed points (policy arms) offline.
- At runtime, choose among arms by evaluating a tiny localization objective using cached signals.

Design decisions:
- What are the arms? (e.g., Fast/Full validation profiles; repair vs deny thresholds)
- What are the fixed point weights per mode (strict vs hardened)?
- How do we ensure determinism and avoid heavy math? (table lookup + integer scoring)

Acceptance criteria:
- A specific arm set + scoring rule + offline artifact format.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:40.307674205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:39:52.729439947Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:39:52.729417735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":637,"issue_id":"bd-15q","author":"PinkMill","text":"Design + implementation complete. -{"id":"bd-18qq","title":"EPIC: Testing and Fault Injection Campaign","description":"Comprehensive testing and fault injection campaign to validate FrankenLibC's safety guarantees under adversarial conditions. This epic covers: (1) Fault injection — systematic injection of adversarial patterns (bad pointers, fragmentation storms, thread preemption) to verify the TSM detects and handles them. (2) End-to-end tests — holistic tests that exercise the full TSM pipeline from detection through repair to evidence logging. (3) POSIX conformance — verify all 250 symbols match POSIX specification behavior. Distinct from the existing fuzzing epic (bd-1oz) which focuses on coverage-guided input generation. This epic focuses on structured, deterministic fault patterns that target specific TSM capabilities. Distinct from the CVE arena (bd-1m5) which focuses on known vulnerability patterns. This epic targets theoretical failure modes that may not have CVEs yet.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection testing from Natella et al. (2016). Adversarial testing from Miller et al. (1990). Stress testing from Voas & McGraw (1998) Software Fault Injection.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Fault injection framework: src/testing/fault_injection.rs with pluggable fault patterns.
- E2E test harness: tests/e2e/ directory with scenario-based tests.
- POSIX conformance: tests/conformance/ with per-symbol test files.
- All tests deterministic and CI-executable.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
1. All 7 subtasks completed (3 fault injection + 3 E2E + 1 conformance).
2. Zero false negatives for UAF and double-free detection.
3. Real-world programs run correctly under strict mode.
4. POSIX conformance covers all 250 symbols.
5. All tests pass in CI within 30-minute time budget.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Test results as structured JSON artifacts for CI dashboard integration.
- Evidence ledger dumps from E2E tests archived as release artifacts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:42.311822196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:24:33.809125929Z","close_reason":"Delivered or tracked as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","frankenlibc","security","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":638,"issue_id":"bd-18qq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-18qq.1","title":"Fault injection: adversarial pointer patterns","description":"Implement a structured fault injection framework for adversarial pointer patterns that exercise the TSM's pointer validation pipeline. Patterns to inject: (1) Use-after-free — allocate, free, use. Vary: delay between free and use (0, 1, 100, 10000 operations), allocation size (small/medium/large), arena (same arena reuse vs cross-arena). (2) Dangling pointer — allocate, create alias, free original, use alias. Test alias detection across: stack pointers, heap pointers, mmap'd regions. (3) Wild pointer — generate pointers outside any valid region: NULL+offset, stack-heap gap, kernel space, unaligned. (4) Double-free — free the same pointer twice with varying delays. (5) Overlapping regions — memcpy/memmove with overlapping src/dst. (6) Off-by-one — access one byte past allocation boundary. For each pattern: verify TSM detects in both strict and hardened mode, verify hardened mode produces correct repair, verify evidence ledger records the event with correct classification. Generate test matrix: pattern_type x delay x size x mode -> detected/repaired/logged.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection from Natella et al. (2016). Adversarial testing from Miller et al. (1990) fuzz testing origins. Pointer safety patterns from Szekeres et al. (2013).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FaultInjector<P: PointerPattern>: generic injector parameterized by adversarial pattern type.
- PointerPattern enum { Uaf { delay: usize }, Dangling { alias_type: AliasType }, Wild { offset: isize }, DoubleFree { delay: usize }, Overlap { bytes: usize }, OffByOne { direction: Direction } }.
- Test matrix: generate all (pattern, size, mode) combinations via proptest or explicit enumeration.
- TSM verification: after injection, query evidence ledger for detection + classification entries.
- Repair verification (hardened): check return value matches expected POSIX error code.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 6 pointer pattern types implemented with at least 3 parameter variations each.
2. UAF detected in both strict and hardened mode with zero false negatives (1000 trials).
3. Double-free detected with zero false negatives (1000 trials, varying delay 0-10000 ops).
4. Wild pointer detected for all tested offsets (NULL+1, stack-heap gap, kernel space).
5. Evidence ledger contains correct classification for each detected fault.
6. Hardened mode produces correct POSIX return for each fault type (documented mapping).
7. Test matrix report: pattern_type x param x mode -> detected/repaired/logged as JSON artifact.

 {"id":"bd-18qq.2","title":"Fault injection: allocator fragmentation storms","description":"Implement fault injection for allocator fragmentation storms — workloads designed to maximally fragment the heap and stress the allocator's ability to maintain performance and safety under pathological allocation patterns. Storm patterns: (1) Sawtooth — allocate N blocks of increasing size, free every other one, repeat. Creates a fragmented free list with many small holes. (2) Inverse sawtooth — allocate small blocks, free them in reverse order of size. Tests coalescing logic. (3) Random churn — randomly allocate and free blocks of random sizes with 50/50 probability. Run for 1M operations. (4) Size-class thrash — rapidly alternate between allocations of different size classes to stress size-class cache invalidation. (5) Arena exhaustion — allocate until arena is full, then free all and reallocate. Test arena recovery. (6) Alignment stress — request allocations with unusual alignments (4096, 65536, 2MB) mixed with normal allocations. For each pattern: measure fragmentation ratio (wasted/total), peak RSS, allocation latency p99, and verify no memory corruption via full heap scan after storm. If SOS fragmentation certificate is implemented, verify it correctly predicts fragmentation levels.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Allocator stress testing from Berger et al. (2001) Hoard. Fragmentation analysis from Johnstone & Wilson (1998). Pathological workloads from Robson (1971).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FragmentationStorm enum { Sawtooth, InverseSawtooth, RandomChurn, SizeClassThrash, ArenaExhaustion, AlignmentStress }.
- Each storm implemented as Iterator<Item = AllocOp> where AllocOp is Alloc(size, align) or Free(ptr).
- Fragmentation measurement: HeapScanner that walks arena metadata, computes wasted/total ratio.
- Post-storm integrity: HeapIntegrityCheck that verifies no corruption (free list consistency, metadata validity).
- SOS certificate correlation: if bd-2ste.1 is implemented, verify certificate prediction matches actual fragmentation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 6 storm patterns implemented and executable as cargo test --test fragmentation_storms.
2. No memory corruption after any storm (heap integrity check passes).
3. Fragmentation ratio measured and stays below 50% for all storms (configurable threshold).
4. Peak RSS stays within 2x of theoretical minimum for each storm pattern.
5. Allocation latency p99 stays below 1us during storm (no pathological slowdown).
6. SOS certificate correlation >0.9 (if certificate implemented).
7. Each storm runs for at least 1M operations (sufficient statistical significance).","notes":"Fragmentation storm gate passes strict+hardened with rch-offloaded validation; see  {"id":"bd-18qq.3","title":"Fault injection: thread preemption storms","description":"Implement fault injection for thread preemption storms — workloads that maximize the probability of preemption at critical points in the TSM validation pipeline and allocator. Techniques: (1) High thread count (128+ threads) with small quanta (sched_setscheduler SCHED_RR with 1ms quantum). (2) Signal injection — send SIGUSR1 at random intervals to force preemption during critical sections. (3) CPU affinity pinning — force all threads onto 1-2 cores to maximize contention and preemption. (4) Priority inversion — create high/medium/low priority threads where medium holds a lock needed by high. (5) Thundering herd — wake all threads simultaneously (pthread_cond_broadcast) while they compete for the same allocation arena. For each pattern: verify no deadlocks (all threads complete within timeout), no data corruption (heap integrity check after storm), no lost operations (all allocations paired with frees), no livelock (progress guarantee). Measure: p99 latency under storm conditions, maximum lock hold time, number of TSM state transitions triggered by preemption.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Concurrency stress testing from Musuvathi et al. (2008) CHESS. Priority inversion from Sha et al. (1990). Preemption testing from Sen (2008).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- PreemptionStorm struct { thread_count: usize, quantum_us: u64, signal_interval_us: u64, affinity_cores: Vec<usize> }.
- SIGUSR1 injection: separate timer thread sending SIGUSR1 to all worker threads at random intervals.
- CPU affinity: use sched_setaffinity to pin all threads to 1-2 cores for maximum preemption.
- Priority inversion: create 3 threads with SCHED_RR priorities high/medium/low, lock ordering to trigger inversion.
- Thundering herd: pthread_cond_broadcast to wake 128 threads competing for same arena.
- Timeout: each storm has configurable timeout (default 30s). If threads do not complete, report deadlock.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 5 preemption patterns implemented and executable.
2. No deadlocks: all patterns complete within timeout (30 seconds).
3. No data corruption: heap integrity verified after each storm.
4. No lost operations: all allocations paired with frees (leak check).
5. No livelock: measurable progress in every 1-second interval.
6. p99 latency under storm < 10x baseline (bounded slowdown under extreme contention).
7. Results reproducible: same storm parameters produce completion within 2x time variance.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per storm: storm_type, thread_count, completion_time_ms, deadlock_detected, corruption_detected, ops_completed.
- tracing::warn!(target: preemption_storm, potential_deadlock, elapsed_ms) if progress stalls.
- Summary: preemption_storm_report.json with all storm results and per-thread operation counts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:12.936668102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:38:35.441117284Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:38:35.440843716Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic preemption-storm suite, gate, and CI wiring; bead-specific gate passes via rch, workspace check passes, repo-wide fmt/clippy still blocked by pre-existing unistd_abi drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.3","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

1) In raw_clock_gettime (line 102-105), if vdso_clock_gettime returns an unexpected POSITIVE rc (buggy vDSO or future ABI change), the current code computes set_abi_errno(-rc) which yields a negative errno value. Per POSIX errno must be positive. Fix: guard with rc < 0 check before negating, or treat any non-{0, -ENOSYS} result as a kernel anomaly warranting fallback.

2) resolve_vdso_symbols (line 166, 178) hardcodes c"LINUX_2.6" as the dlvsym version. x86_64's vDSO uses LINUX_2.6, but aarch64 uses LINUX_2.6.39 and riscv64 uses LINUX_4.15. Currently falls back to dlsym (unversioned) on non-x86_64 — works but costs a wasted dlvsym call. Fix: per-arch version constants.
